stories.title: Beachum Watch — Day 14, Final 24 Hours
stories.angle: Jaysoni Beachum (.417 BA / 16 HR / 66 RBI / .549 OBP / career 37 HR / 180 RBI / 202 H) is entering the final 24 hours of her transfer portal decision. The Softball America No. 1 rated transfer has visited the Florida Gators and is weighing her options. Portal closes Monday, June 22. She is a Tallahassee native potentially heading to FSU's rival. Every program still in pursuit has one day left to make their case. The clock has almost run out.

x_posts.label: Post 1
x_posts.text:
The No. 1 transfer in college softball still hasn't committed.

Jaysoni Beachum. .417 BA. 16 HR. 66 RBI. One year eligible.

Portal closes TOMORROW (June 22). She visited Florida.

Final 24 hours. ⏰

#Softball #TransferPortal

x_posts.label: Post 2
x_posts.text:
Jaysoni Beachum is a Tallahassee native who played at FSU.

She visited the Florida GATORS.

If she picks UF, it's one of the most dramatic in-state flips in college softball history.

Final 24 hours. 🔥

#FSUSoftball #FloridaSoftball

facebook.long_form:
The most-watched transfer in college softball history has one day left to decide.

Jaysoni Beachum — the Softball America No. 1 rated portal player — still hasn't committed on Day 14 of the 15-day transfer window. The portal closes Monday, June 22. Tomorrow is the deadline.

Beachum's 2026 season at Florida State was remarkable: a .417 batting average, 16 home runs, 66 RBI, and a .549 on-base percentage that ranked third in FSU program history. She drew 54 walks — a testament to how much opposing coaches feared seeing her name in the lineup. She earned NFCA Second-Team All-American honors and was a cornerstone of the Seminoles' offense.

Now the Tallahassee native is leaving the school she grew up near and may be heading to the in-state rival. She visited the Florida Gators — the program that has long been FSU's most heated rival in softball. For FSU fans, that visit stung. For Florida fans, the possibility of landing the No. 1 transfer would be a massive recruiting win heading into 2027.

With one year of eligibility remaining, Beachum brings immediate impact wherever she lands. She hits for power, draws walks at an elite rate, and plays premium defense at third base. Any program that lands her becomes a legitimate 2027 threat overnight.

Whatever happens in the next 24 hours — this has been a remarkable portal saga. And it ends tomorrow.
facebook.image_caption: Final 24 hours for the No. 1 transfer in college softball. Jaysoni Beachum decides by tomorrow. 🥎 #Softball #TransferPortal

article.headline: Beachum Watch — Day 14: The No. 1 Transfer Has One Day Left to Decide

stories.title: South Carolina Adds Bella Foran — Gold Glove Outfielder Bolsters Gamecocks
stories.angle: South Carolina softball added Gold Glove outfielder Bella Foran on June 20 as programs made final-day portal pushes. Foran's commitment gives the Gamecocks an elite defensive piece in the outfield heading into 2027. With the portal closing tomorrow, South Carolina has been active in the final stretch. Their haul — including Foran and Tennessee transfer Saviya Morgan — makes them one of the portal's quieter winners.

x_posts.label: Post 1
x_posts.text:
South Carolina softball just landed Gold Glove outfielder Bella Foran. 🔥

Add her to Saviya Morgan (Tennessee transfer) and the Gamecocks are quietly building something dangerous for 2027.

Don't sleep on South Carolina. #GarnetAndBlack #Softball

facebook.long_form:
South Carolina softball is quietly having one of the best transfer portal windows in the country.

The Gamecocks landed Gold Glove outfielder Bella Foran on June 20 — adding an elite defensive piece to a portal haul that already included Tennessee transfer Saviya Morgan. With the portal window closing tomorrow, South Carolina has emerged as one of the under-the-radar winners of the 2026 cycle.

Morgan, a top-ranked outfielder from Tennessee, gives South Carolina immediate offensive production in the outfield. Foran adds Gold Glove-caliber defense to a lineup that should be significantly improved in 2027. Together, they represent a calculated and effective approach to the portal — targeting complementary pieces that fill specific needs.

South Carolina wasn't the biggest name in this portal window. Texas Tech and Texas dominated the headlines with their historic hauls. But the Gamecocks did exactly what a program needs to do in the portal era: identify their needs and fill them with quality players who fit.

Keep an eye on South Carolina in 2027. They just got better in a big way.
facebook.image_caption: South Carolina landed Gold Glove outfielder Bella Foran to close out a strong portal window. The Gamecocks are building something for 2027. 🥎 #GarnetAndBlack #Softball

article.headline: South Carolina Lands Bella Foran — Gamecocks Quietly Win the Transfer Portal

stories.title: FSU Adds Second Portal Piece — Edmiaston Joins Dodge in Seminoles' Rebuild
stories.angle: Florida State isn't done rebuilding. After landing Tennessee transfer Ella Dodge (No. 10 overall, No. 4 MI), the Seminoles have now added former Stetson first baseman Nicole Edmiaston to the portal haul. FSU lost Isa Torres (to Texas) and is still waiting on Beachum — but the roster construction is taking shape. Two portal additions plus six incoming freshmen give head coach Lonni Alameda a foundation to work with heading into 2027.

x_posts.label: Post 1
x_posts.text:
FSU softball is rebuilding — one piece at a time.

After landing Ella Dodge (No. 10 portal / Tennessee), the Seminoles added Nicole Edmiaston from Stetson at first base.

Two portal additions. Six incoming freshmen. The rebuild is underway. #FSUSoftball #Softball

facebook.long_form:
Florida State lost two of the best players in program history to the transfer portal. Now they're putting the pieces back together.

The Seminoles have added former Stetson first baseman Nicole Edmiaston as their second portal piece of the 2026 window. She joins Tennessee transfer Ella Dodge — the No. 10 overall portal player and No. 4 middle infielder — who committed to FSU last week.

Let's put FSU's situation in context. Isa Torres, the D1 Softball Player of the Year with a .530 batting average, left for two-time defending champion Texas. Jaysoni Beachum, the No. 1 transfer and a .417 hitter, is still weighing her options with one day left in the portal. Those are massive losses for any program to absorb.

But head coach Lonni Alameda isn't starting from scratch. The Seminoles return strong core pieces and are adding six incoming freshmen alongside their two portal additions. Dodge gives FSU a two-year player with positional versatility and power (11 HR in 2026). Edmiaston adds depth and power at first base.

Is FSU an immediate contender in 2027? The losses are significant. But with Alameda at the helm and a recruitment class taking shape, the Seminoles are building toward something. This portal window is the foundation, not the finished product.
facebook.image_caption: FSU adds Nicole Edmiaston as their second portal piece. The Seminoles are rebuilding after losing Torres and potentially Beachum — and Alameda has a plan. 🥎 #FSUSoftball #Softball

article.headline: FSU Adds Edmiaston as Second Portal Piece — Seminoles Rebuild Takes Shape

stories.title: Portal Winner Final Verdict — Who Built the Best 2027 Roster?
stories.angle: With one day left, the 2026 transfer portal verdict is nearly sealed. Texas (Torres + Swan + Lincoln + Altmeyer) brought in a generational class. Texas Tech (Polar + Heavener + Pickering) assembled three All-Americans from a 61-8 runner-up. Arkansas (Wetteland + Yarnell) built depth. South Carolina (Morgan + Foran) had a strong quiet finish. Tennessee (Shelton + Mardjetko) retained key pieces. FSU added Dodge + Edmiaston. With 84+ players committed out of 120, the portal reshaped the 2027 landscape.

x_posts.label: Post 1
x_posts.text:
With one day left in the portal, it comes down to two:

🤘 Texas: Torres + Swan + Lincoln — a generational class
🤘 Texas Tech: Polar + Heavener + Pickering — three All-Americans

Who won the portal? #Softball #TransferPortal #LonghornSoftball

x_posts.label: Post 2
x_posts.text:
Portal closes tomorrow — some programs flew under the radar:

✅ South Carolina: Morgan + Foran
✅ Arkansas: Wetteland + Yarnell
✅ FSU: Dodge + Edmiaston
✅ Tennessee: Shelton + kept Mardjetko

84 of 120 portal players committed. 2027 is taking shape. #Softball #TransferPortal

facebook.long_form:
With one day left in the 2026 transfer portal, the verdict is nearly in. And it comes down to two programs — with a few others making strong cases.

**Texas** made the boldest moves of anyone. Adding D1 Softball Player of the Year Isa Torres (a Georgetown, TX native coming home) alongside Swan (All-Big 12 catcher from the same hometown), Lincoln, and Altmeyer gives two-time defending champion Texas arguably the most talented transfer class in college softball history. If Torres transitions seamlessly from FSU to Austin, the Longhorns are the prohibitive favorite for a three-peat.

**Texas Tech** made history of its own. The 61-8 runner-up assembled three All-Americans: Kenna Polar (.555 BA / 101 hits), Heavener (the nation's top-ranked pitcher), and Mackenzie Pickering. Softball America rated their class No. 1. The Red Raiders went from WCWS runner-up to potential title favorite in the span of two weeks.

**Arkansas** had a strong, efficient window. Landing Mountain West Freshman of the Year Madison Wetteland and UCF utility player Kendall Yarnell gives Dave Engel quality depth and versatility.

**South Carolina** closed strong. Saviya Morgan (Tennessee OF) and Bella Foran (Gold Glove OF) make the Gamecocks an improved team heading into 2027.

**Tennessee** played defense smartly — adding South Carolina second baseman Shelton while retaining senior pitcher Mardjetko.

**FSU** starts their rebuild with Ella Dodge and Nicole Edmiaston.

84 of 120 portal players are committed. One name — Beachum — remains. Whoever gets her claims the final headline of the 2026 portal.
facebook.image_caption: With one day left, Texas and Texas Tech stand alone as the portal's biggest winners. But the full picture comes into focus tomorrow when the window closes. 🥎 #Softball #TransferPortal

article.headline: Who Won the 2026 Transfer Portal? The Final Verdict With One Day Left

stories.title: Portal Tracker — Day 14 of 15, Last 24 Hours
stories.angle: Day 14 of 15. One day remains before the June 22 portal closes. The committed class is 84+ of 120 players (70%). Notable final-stretch commitments: Foran (SC), Morgan (SC), Edmiaston (FSU), Foran (SC). The one name still missing: Beachum. She has visited Florida and the clock is ticking. Whatever happens with Beachum in the next 24 hours will define the final chapter of the 2026 portal.

x_posts.label: Post 1
x_posts.text:
🚨 Transfer portal — Day 14 of 15

84+ of 120 committed (70%)
Portal closes TOMORROW, June 22

Still out: Beachum (No. 1) 👀

Final 24 hours will define the last chapter of the 2026 portal. #Softball #TransferPortal

facebook.long_form:
Day 14 of 15. The 2026 softball transfer portal closes tomorrow at midnight.

Here's where things stand heading into the final 24 hours:

📊 **By the numbers:** 120+ players entered the portal. 84+ have committed (70%). The window opened June 8 and closes June 22.

🏆 **Power programs accounted for:**
- Texas: Torres, Swan, Lincoln, Altmeyer ✅
- Texas Tech: Polar, Heavener, Pickering ✅
- Arkansas: Wetteland, Yarnell ✅
- South Carolina: Morgan, Foran ✅
- Tennessee: Shelton + retained Mardjetko ✅
- Texas A&M: Azua ✅
- Alabama: Plumlee ✅
- FSU: Dodge, Edmiaston ✅
- Ohio State: Hartley ✅

👀 **Still out:**
- Jaysoni Beachum (No. 1 — visited Florida)

That's it. One name. The biggest name. And she has until tomorrow to decide.

Whatever Beachum does in the next 24 hours is the final chapter of the 2026 transfer portal. Follow along for updates.
facebook.image_caption: Day 14 of 15. 84 of 120 committed. One name left. Beachum decides tomorrow. The 2026 portal closes Monday. 🥎 #Softball #TransferPortal
